title: Employee Benefits Dashboard — Personalized Home Screen
description: The system shall provide an employee-specific Benefits Dashboard as the home screen after login for the Employee persona. The dashboard displays the employee's identity, active enrollment status across all benefit types, and primary action shortcuts.
demoPrimary: Linda White — EMP-10045 — Active — Benefits Effective Jan 1, 2027

title: My Benefits — Employee Current Coverage View
description: The Employee Portal shall provide a My Benefits screen listing all active benefit enrollments as a summary table. Each row is selectable to expand a detail view showing plan specifics, deductible, out-of-pocket maximum, employer contribution, employee premium, coverage tier, and which dependents are covered.

title: My Elections — Current and Historical Election History
description: The Employee Portal shall allow employees to view their current plan elections and election history organized by plan year. Each election record shows the election date, effective date, enrollment type, plan version at time of election, and status.
electionRecordFields: Enrollment Type (Open Enrollment / Life Event / New Hire)

title: My Deductions — Payroll Deduction Summary and History
description: The Employee Portal shall provide a My Deductions screen showing the employee's current per-pay-period deduction breakdown by benefit type, a running total, and a historical deduction ledger. All four payroll frequencies (weekly, biweekly, semi-monthly, monthly) must be supported and displayed correctly.

currentDeductionDisplay.lineItems: One row per benefit with plan name and employee premium amount
currentDeductionDisplay.total: Sum of all benefit deductions per pay period
currentDeductionDisplay.payFrequencyLabel: Displayed below total (e.g. 'per biweekly pay period')

aiUseCase.query: Why did my payroll deduction increase this month?
aiUseCase.response: Your medical deduction increased from $125 to $150 because your 2027 PPO 500 employee premium became effective January 1. This was the result of the plan year renewal approved on November 15, 2026.

title: My Documents — Employee Benefit Document Center
description: The Employee Portal shall provide a My Documents screen listing all benefit-related documents available to the employee. Each document supports inline HTML preview and PDF download. The document list is scoped to the employee's own documents only — no other employees' documents are accessible.
accessScope: Employee's own documents only. HR Admin and Benefits Admin may view on behalf.

title: Benefit Summary PDF — Personalized Per-Employee Generated Document
description: The system shall generate a personalized benefit summary PDF for each employee that summarizes their current benefit elections, coverage levels, dependents, effective dates, premiums, employer contributions, and per-pay-period deduction total. The PDF is generated on demand and made available in My Documents.
pdfSections: Employer branding header (Acme Corporation + plan year)
Employee information (name, ID, status, benefits effective date)
Benefit elections table (benefit / plan / coverage tier / effective date / status)
Premium summary (employee premium / employer contribution per benefit)
generationTrigger: On demand from My Documents; also generated automatically on enrollment confirmation
demoNote: Demo generates a real PDF using seeded data for Linda White (EMP-10045)

title: Life Event Self-Service — AI-Assisted Life Event Reporting
description: The Employee Portal shall allow employees to report a qualifying life event via a structured form or via the AI Benefits Assistant. The AI assistant accepts natural language input, identifies the event type, confirms the event date, states the enrollment window deadline, lists required documentation, and provides a [Start Enrollment] call-to-action.
aiAssistantFlow: AI: 'Congratulations! When did you get married?'
AI confirms: Life Event = Marriage | Event Date = June 15 | Enrollment Deadline = July 15 | Status = Eligible | Required docs: marriage certificate
handoff: AI collects event type + date via conversation then hands off to the structured life event enrollment workflow

title: Employee Enrollment — Self-Service Enrollment Wizard in Employee Portal
description: The Employee Portal shall allow eligible employees to complete benefit enrollment independently through the enrollment wizard. The wizard follows the same 8-step flow defined in REQ-ENROLL-UX-001–015, but rendered within the employee's portal context. HR Admin and Benefits Admin can complete enrollment on behalf of an employee.
wizardSteps: Review Premium — cost breakdown before confirming
onBehalfOf: HR Admin and Benefits Admin may complete enrollment on behalf of an employee; the employee's explicit confirmation is required for self-service enrollment

title: AI Benefits Assistant — Employee-Scoped Conversational Assistant
description: The Employee Portal shall provide an AI Benefits Assistant accessible from the dashboard and from any portal screen. The assistant answers natural language questions about the employee's own benefits, explains deduction changes, compares plans for the employee's situation, and can initiate enrollment or life event workflows.
exampleQueries: What benefits do I currently have?
How much do I pay for medical coverage?
What happens if I get married?
What is the difference between PPO 500 and HDHP 3000?
Why is my dependent not covered?
responseFormat: Answer + Source citation (document, page) + Relevant Action button (e.g. [View Policy], [Continue Enrollment], [Update Dependent])

Recommend: Which plan is best for me?

dataScope: Employee's own benefits, elections, deductions, dependents, and published plan summaries only — never other employees' data or draft configurations

title: Persona Switch — Admin-to-Employee View Context Switch for Demo
description: The demo shall support a persona switch from Benefits Administrator to a specific employee (Linda White) that immediately renders the Employee Portal in the context of that employee's data. The persona switch is accessible from the global navigation header and the Demo Control Center.
switchFlow: Benefits Administrator view → [Switch Persona] → Employee: Linda White
Renders: Employee Benefits Dashboard for EMP-10045
Scoped to: Linda White's benefits, elections, deductions, documents, AI assistant
demoNarrative: Admin finds Linda White's enrollment exception → switches to Linda's view → employee sees the same exception from their own perspective → AI assistant explains the issue → employee corrects dependent information

title: Employee Portal Role Capability Matrix
description: The Employee Portal enforces the following capability matrix. UI hides unauthorized actions; API enforces access restrictions independently.

title: Demo Flow Steps 10–13 — Employee Portal Closes the End-to-End Loop
description: The master demo script shall include a four-step employee portal sequence (Steps 10–13) that follows the administrator's carrier rejection discovery, switches to the employee's view, uses the AI assistant to diagnose the issue, and demonstrates the full resolution cycle through to carrier confirmation.

actions: Identify exception: Dependent SSN Invalid → CT-10045 carrier rejection
Linda asks: 'Why is my dependent not covered?'
AI identifies invalid SSN on DEP-022 (Sarah Smith)

narrativeClose: Full end-to-end AI-powered employee journey: PDF → REQ → RULE → PLAN → ENROLLMENT → CARRIER TX → PAYROLL → EMPLOYEE CONFIRMATION

title: AI Benefits Assistant — Explain / Recommend / Act Framework
description: The AI Benefits Assistant in the Employee Portal shall operate across three interaction modes: Explain (answer questions about current benefits, deductions, and eligibility), Recommend (compare plans, suggest elections based on profile), and Act (initiate enrollment, report a life event, update dependent, download summary). Every AI response includes a source citation and a contextually relevant action button.

examples: Which plan is best for my family?
Recommend.responseIncludes: Ranked recommendation + tradeoff explanation + plan comparison table

Act.responseIncludes: Action button that initiates the relevant workflow directly from the AI response

governancePrinciple: AI explains and recommends; employee confirms all enrollment actions explicitly. AI cannot auto-enroll.

title: Employee Portal Access is Scoped — Employee Cannot Access Other Employees' Data
description: All Employee Portal API endpoints shall enforce employee-scoped data access. An authenticated employee may only retrieve their own enrollment records, deductions, documents, and dependent information. Attempting to access another employee's data returns HTTP 403 regardless of any query parameter manipulation. This is enforced at the API layer independently of the UI.
apiEnforcement: Server reads employeeId from the authenticated token — never from a request body or query param — and applies it as a mandatory filter on all employee-scoped queries

title: Enrollment Confirmation Screen — Post-Submission Summary with PDF Download
description: After the employee submits enrollment, the system shall display an enrollment confirmation screen summarizing all elected benefits, covered dependents, the per-pay-period deduction total, the effective date, and a confirmation reference number. The employee can immediately download their Benefit Summary PDF from this screen.
confirmationScreenSections: Confirmation reference number and submission timestamp
Elected benefits summary table (benefit / plan / coverage tier / effective date)
Next steps (e.g. 'Your carrier will be notified. Your coverage is effective January 1, 2027.')
